Jiayi Wu
Home Projects

Pattern Mining and Automated Tactic Discovery in Lean4 Theorem Proving

Research project advised by Robert Lewis and Stephen Bach at Brown University Department of Computer Science

Motif of Thoughts (MoT): Reusable Abstractions for Neurosymbolic Reasoning

Research project in collaboration with Chance Jiajie Li et al.

  • Simulating Society Requires Simulating Thought [Preprint], accepted to Conference on Neural Information Processing Systems (NeurIPS) 2025, Position Paper Track
  • HugAgent: Evaluating LLMs in Simulating Human-Like Individual Reasoning on Open-Ended Tasks [Preprint] [Repository], accepted to NeurIPS 2025 Workshop on
    • LAW 2025: Bridging Language, Agent, and World Models Spotlight
    • PersonaLLM: Workshop on LLM Persona Modeling Oral
    • Socially Responsible and Trustworthy Foundation Models (ResponsibleFM)
  • …

Formalizing and Benchmarking Overton Pluralistic Alignment in Large Language Model Evaluation

Visiting summer researcher project in collaboration with Elinor Poole-Dayan et al., advised by Michiel Bakker at Massachusetts Institute of Technology

  • Benchmarking Overton Pluralism in LLMs [Preprint], accepted to NeurIPS 2025 Workshop on Evaluating the Evolving LLM Lifecycle: Benchmarks, Emergent Abilities, and Scaling
  • …

Pluggable Analyses for Modern Real Systems

Undergraduate Teaching and Research Awards (UTRA) project advised by Nikos Vasilakis at ATLAS Group, Brown University Department of Computer Science

AI Policy Glossary

Research project advised by Sarah Cen at Carnegie Mellon University Department of Electrical & Computer Engineering (ECE) and Engineering & Public Policy (EPP)

Formalizing Hartshorne’s Foundations of Projective Geometry in Isabelle [Repository]

Class final project at CSCI 1951Y Using an Interactive Proof Assistant to Do Mathematics, Brown University Department of Computer Science

Algorithmic Fairness and AI Governance Primer, Socially Responsible Computing (SRC) Handbook [Handbook]

Research project advised by Suresh Venkatasubramanian and Julia Netter at Brown University Department of Computer Science and Data Science Institute’s Center for Tech Responsibility (CNTR)

Trade-Off Modeling and Auditing Formalization in Platform-Based Algorithmic Management: A Multi-Objective Optimization Approach

Research project at The Data in Society Collective (DISCO Lab), advised by Harini Suresh; previously class final project at CSCI 2952W Critical Data and Machine Learning, Brown University Department of Computer Science
